behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real STT model output content (speaker diarization/label preservation), so mocked transcripts can hide real attribution loss (LLM-DEP and MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels" (single model stage), not a required multi-agentic sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with multiple speakers through the production STT API, then assert transcript chunks retain stable speaker labels mapped to distinct attendees; fail if labels collapse, drift, or are missing before summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic over transcript text to structured fields; the failure boundary is local extraction rules (MOCK-MISS says one-function parsing remains Unit). Rejected Integration: no quoted requirement for real module handoff/IO; behavior text is specifically "Transcript parsing extracts... into the summary input structure."
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted transcript strings (including edge phrasing and multiple assignees) into the parsing function and assert exact assignee/task tuples in the intermediate summary-input object.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Plausibly requires chained agentic stages in live flow (assignment mention in meeting -> transcript -> summary action-item generation), and correctness depends on model decisions about inclusion/coverage (LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP). By bidirectional gate, Agentic and Workflow are both plausible; choose higher-risk Workflow because behavior is about "live summary output" for assignments "during the meeting," implying end-to-end sequential stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real meeting recording with known per-attendee assignments, run full live pipeline, and assert each attendee with an assignment appears with at least one corresponding action item in final structured output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Kept Workflow because the assertion is on end output after sequential STT+LLM stages, not a single isolated model call.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior scopes to routing logic after decisions are already "identified"; this is deterministic field-mapping logic at a local boundary. Rejected Integration: quoted text disqualifies higher levels because it tests routing of already identified items, not real infra or model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Given an internal object containing identified decision items plus narrative text, run the routing function and assert decision entries land only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment to extract and place salient decisions from transcript text; stubs would always pass routing expectations and hide misses (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow: behavior text starts from "mentioned in the transcript" and checks one summary output placement, so multi-agentic sequencing is not explicitly required.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcript inputs containing explicit and implicit decisions to the production summarization model and assert those decisions appear in final `decisions` entries rather than only free-form narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step sequencing: "splits... into segments," "processes them sequentially," then "merging" into one coherent summary (MULTI-STEP). Also coherence depends on real model outputs across segments, so lower mocked levels can miss cross-segment degradation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production pipeline, verify segment boundaries and ordered processing events, then assert merged final summary preserves cross-segment continuity (no dropped/duplicated decisions or action items).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly end-to-end across multiple stages including real model calls and delivery: "full pipeline... for a real meeting recording." This is the definition of workflow-level failure boundary, not a single stage. Rejected System: real infra is necessary but insufficient because success depends on multiple sequential agentic outputs, so Workflow is higher required level.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an unattended production-like run on a real meeting file from upload through delivery endpoint, and assert terminal success plus valid structured payload sections (`action_items`, `decisions`, attendee attributions) without operator retries/manual fixes.
